most_recent_game.recap_text:
BUIES CREEK, N.C.
-
Collin Anderson
hit the third home run of his freshman campaign but Duke fell at Campbell, 13-3, on Tuesday night at Jim Perry Stadium. The Blue Devils (15-8) fell behind early, 3-0, and pulled within a run, 3-2, but the Camels (10-9) scored the final 10 runs of the contest to secure the victory.
Anderson homered in the fifth inning, his third time going yard this season. The second baseman also drove in a run in the sixth frame, absorbing a pitch with the bases loaded, increasing his season RBI total to nine.
HOW IT HAPPENED
Campbell got on the board first, scoring on a wild pitch in the first inning. A sacrifice fly by the Camels in the second frame doubled Campbell's lead.
Duke cut the deficit in half with a sacrifice fly by
Kaden Smith
in the top of the third, bringing home
Tyler Albright
.
After the Camels used another sacrifice fly to drive in their third run of the night,
Collin Anderson
blasted a home run to left field on the first pitch he saw to start the top half of the fifth.
Campbell put up six runs in the bottom half of the inning and Anderson was hit by a pitch with the bases loaded in the sixth, his second RBI of the night.
The Camels scratched across a run in the bottom of the sixth off a wild pitch and ended the game in the eighth with three runs to go up by 10 runs, 13-3.
UP NEXT
Duke remains away from home for its first ACC road test of the season, traveling to Virginia Tech. The Blue Devils and Hokies will play three games March 20-22 at English Field at Atlantic Union Bank Park.
First pitch on Friday is scheduled for 6 p.m., followed by a 3 p.m. start time on Saturday and a 1 p.m. finale on Sunday.
For more information on Duke baseball, follow the Blue Devils on
Twitter
,
Facebook
and
Instagram
by searching for "DukeBASE".
#GoDuke

fafe023d.name: Jeff Lougee
fafe023d.narrative: Limited opportunities have defined Lougee's early season arc—benched for Tuesday's loss to High Point, he recorded just one plate appearance in a game Duke couldn't recover from after surrendering six first-inning runs. Through two games, the sophomore infielder remains hitless with no RBI, signaling he's yet to break into the Blue Devils' primary lineup rotation.

1082d2c4.name: Adin Zorn
1082d2c4.narrative: In a shutout loss to Miami, Zorn went 1-for-3 with two strikeouts and a walk, unable to contribute in an offensive drought that saw Duke manage only scattered baserunners despite four double plays turned defensively. Now 16 games into his junior season, the outfielder sits at a .196 average with just one home run and nine RBIs, signaling a concerning start that demands a significant offensive adjustment moving forward.

82657e7c.name: Coltin Quagliano
82657e7c.narrative: 2/3 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .308 with 0 HR through 22 games.

ea475a8a.name: Tyler Albright
ea475a8a.narrative: 1/3 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .310 with 1 HR through 22 games.

8478bd88.name: Matthew Strand
8478bd88.narrative: Strand delivered a two-homer, six-RBI performance anchored by a first-inning grand slam to power Duke's 20-run offensive explosion against Miami, the program's first 20-run ACC game since 1992. Through 13 games, the junior backstop has posted a .304 average with 4 homers and 12 RBI, demonstrating increased power production as the Blue Devils chase their first ACC series win of the season.

156b7173.name: Jake Lambdin
156b7173.narrative: 0/3 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .256 with 4 HR through 23 games.

dce3bddb.name: Jordan Regulski
dce3bddb.narrative: 1.1 IP, 0 K vs. Campbell (L). At 16.46 ERA through 8.2 innings on the season.

ce2ee4c7.name: RJ Hamilton
ce2ee4c7.narrative: 1/4 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .343 with 1 HR through 20 games.

d9cc0d8b.name: Ben Petrone
d9cc0d8b.narrative: 0/4 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .235 with 0 HR through 8 games.

95eb4bd3.name: Aidan Weaver
95eb4bd3.narrative: Senior RHP dominated Miami with a six-inning shutout Friday, needing just 89 pitches to strike out eight while allowing two hits—his third start this spring with 8+ K's and two or fewer runs. The 4-1 record and 3.64 ERA over 27.2 IP positions Weaver as a reliable weekend starter for Duke's ACC tournament push heading into the final month.

06324faf.name: Edward Hart
06324faf.narrative: Limited to 1.1 IP in a conference-opening loss to Notre Dame, Hart yielded two hits and a run while striking out one in a short outing that underscored Duke's bullpen depth against a ranked opponent. Early-season workload remains modest at 3.2 IP across appearances, though his 2.81 ERA and 5 K's suggest effective stuff when given opportunities to pitch deeper into games.

5f13c6f4.name: Andrew Bell
5f13c6f4.narrative: In a dominant 20-3 victory over Milwaukee, Bell appeared in limited action as part of Duke's explosive offensive display that featured five different multi-RBI contributors and a pitching performance anchored by Aidan Weaver's nine strikeouts through five innings. Through five games, the sophomore infielder remains hitless at the plate with no RBIs, signaling early developmental concerns despite the team's fast 5-1 start to the season.

f828c713.name: Trevor Johnson
f828c713.narrative: In his second game of the season, Johnson went hitless against Notre Dame's Jack Radel, striking out in limited action as Duke's offense struggled early before mounting a late rally in the 10-4 conference loss. The sophomore has yet to record a hit through two games, leaving him with a .200 average and no RBIs as the Blue Devils begin their ACC schedule.

601a9476.name: Bobby Marsh
601a9476.narrative: Marsh went hitless in three at-bats with two strikeouts against High Point, extending struggles in a Duke lineup that couldn't recover from a six-run first-inning deficit in the 12-6 loss. Through 15 games, the senior first baseman/outfielder is hitting just .212 with 2 homers and 10 RBI, suggesting diminished production in what appears to be a final collegiate season.

63deb6ff.name: Cider Canon
63deb6ff.narrative: In Duke's 20-10 run-rule victory over Miami, Canon worked a walk in limited opportunities as part of a Blue Devils offensive explosion that featured six different players driving in multiple runs and a program-tying 20-run outburst in conference play. Through 14 games, the junior is hitting .452 with 6 home runs and 21 RBI, establishing himself as a consistent run-producer in Duke's high-octane lineup.

a2be0cdb.name: Anthony Runfola
a2be0cdb.narrative: 0/4 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .000 with 0 HR through 3 games.

b7f486cd.name: Michael DiMartini
b7f486cd.narrative: Despite Duke's shutout loss to Miami, DiMartini recorded the game's lone extra-base hit with a double and aggressive baserunning (stealing third), showing offensive spark in an otherwise dormant lineup. The junior's .179 average through 21 games reflects a prolonged slump that has limited his impact to just 2 home runs and 12 RBI on the season.

4da5e11a.name: Adam Troch
4da5e11a.narrative: In Duke's 20-10 run-rule victory over Miami, Troch contributed a walk and scored twice in an offensive explosion anchored by Matthew Strand's pair of homers and six RBIs. Through 11 games, the sophomore outfielder is hitting .294 with modest power production (0 HR, 3 RBI) while demonstrating plate discipline as part of a Blue Devils lineup that's worked 12 walks in this contest alone.

6ae436b7.name: David Boisvert
6ae436b7.narrative: Entered in relief after Duke's starter exited with injury in the first inning, Boisvert navigated 2.1 innings of clean baseball against Miami, allowing just one hit while striking out two in a 9-0 loss. Through early season action, the right-hander has emerged as a reliable bullpen arm with a 0.00 ERA across 4.1 innings pitched and a 9:1 strikeout-to-walk ratio.

a3186d5c.name: Brooks Perez
a3186d5c.narrative: 0/2 with 0 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .212 with 2 HR through 19 games.

627fd26a.name: Nolan Johnson
627fd26a.narrative: Johnson went hitless in four at-bats during Duke's season-opening win over Maine, struggling to make an impact while teammates like Jake Lambdin and RJ Hamilton carried the offensive load with extra-base hits. Through two early-season games, the sophomore backstop has managed just one hit on a .200 average with no RBIs, suggesting a slow start that warrants monitoring as the Blue Devils continue building chemistry under new head coach Corey Muscara.

fe4fa01d.name: Caleb Anderson
fe4fa01d.narrative: A brief mop-up appearance in Duke's 10-4 ACC-opening loss to Notre Dame saw Anderson limited to 1.2 innings with three earned runs allowed on three hits, marking a rough outing as the Blue Devils' bullpen couldn't contain the Fighting Irish's fifth-inning explosion. The right-hander, who has appeared in nine games this season with a .280 batting average and two homers, looks to bounce back as Duke seeks to even the series Saturday.

47fb492e.name: Peter Lemke
47fb492e.narrative: 1.2 IP, 2 K vs. Campbell (L). At 2.80 ERA through 16.1 innings on the season.

82faa2d3.name: Bennett LaPalm
82faa2d3.narrative: Called in to stabilize after starter Matthew Nochowitz exited with a first-inning arm injury, LaPalm recorded just one strikeout across 0.2 innings in Duke's 9-0 shutout loss to Miami, unable to slow the Hurricanes' early offensive barrage. Through seven innings pitched this season, the junior southpaw owns a 3.86 ERA with nine strikeouts, positioning him as a key bullpen asset despite the abbreviated outing against a strong ACC opponent.

7ca13062.name: Matthew Nochowitz
7ca13062.narrative: Throwing arm injury forced an early exit after just 13 pitches in the first inning against Miami, with Nochowitz unable to escape the opening frame as the Hurricanes built an early 3-0 lead. Season-long control issues (9 BB in 17.0 IP) paired with elevated ERA (9.00) now compounded by injury designation heading into the weekend rubber match, raising durability questions for the junior starter.

639e24f2.name: Aiden O'Connell
639e24f2.narrative: Striking out one in 1.2 scoreless innings of relief, O'Connell continued Duke's dominant pitching performance in a 20-10 run-rule victory over Miami, maintaining a spotless ERA across his last three appearances. Through 11.2 IP this season, the sophomore LHP has compiled 17 strikeouts against a 4.02 ERA, establishing himself as a reliable depth option in the Blue Devils' bullpen rotation.

cea9aa42.name: Andy Leon
cea9aa42.narrative: Leon opened Duke's run-rule victory over Miami with a clean first inning on just eight pitches before the offense exploded behind him, though he surrendered six earned runs across 3.1 innings in a game that got away early. Through 20 innings this season, the righty has posted a 4.05 ERA with 20 strikeouts but inconsistent command (3 BB in last outing), signaling a pitcher still working to harness stuff in conference play.

711d90fb.name: Andrew Duncan
711d90fb.narrative: Limited opportunity characterized Duncan's appearance in Duke's 12-6 loss to High Point, recording a pinch-hit at-bat without contact in a game where the Blue Devils' offensive firepower came from Anderson, Lambdin, and Smith. Through two games to start his freshman campaign, Duncan remained without a hit and faces the challenge of breaking into the lineup as Duke (13-6, 1-2 ACC) opens conference play against Miami.

0f0057b8.name: Collin Anderson
0f0057b8.narrative: 1/2 with 2 RBI, 1 HR vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .296 with 3 HR through 10 games.

7b31a3fe.name: Roman DiGiacomo
7b31a3fe.narrative: 1.1 IP, 1 K vs. Campbell (L). At 3.00 ERA through 9.0 innings on the season.

1b90c756.name: Marcello Mastroianni
1b90c756.narrative: 1.0 IP, 0 K vs. Campbell (L). At 4.09 ERA through 13.2 innings on the season.

6f4b08b5.name: Jack Hedrick
6f4b08b5.narrative: Hedrick posted a respectable 2.0 IP outing with 4 K and 2 BB against Miami, though the dominant offensive display (Duke's 20-run effort) overshadowed what was a solid relief appearance in a run-rule victory. Through 17.1 IP this season, the sophomore has maintained a 3.16 ERA with 18 K, establishing himself as a dependable mid-rotation arm for the Blue Devils' ACC campaign.

484fd671.name: Ben Dean
484fd671.narrative: 0.2 IP, 1 K vs. Campbell (L). At 3.68 ERA through 17.1 innings on the season.

401b3ba9.name: Harley Goodner
401b3ba9.narrative: In his first career at-bat, Goodner went deep against Milwaukee in Duke's explosive 20-3 victory, joining teammate Trevor Johnson in launching a homer in their respective debuts. The sophomore infielder remains perfect through one game on the young season with a 1.000 average and 1 HR, positioning himself as a potential depth contributor for the Blue Devils' infield.

10c8d3a0.name: Drew Bryan
10c8d3a0.narrative: 1.0 IP, 0 K vs. Campbell (L). At 6.43 ERA through 4.2 innings on the season.

d94c9d9c.name: Kaden Smith
d94c9d9c.narrative: 0/2 with 1 RBI vs. Campbell (L). Hitting .289 with 3 HR through 11 games.

8cc84cfe.name: Grayson Walker
8cc84cfe.narrative: 0.2 IP, 1 K vs. Campbell (L). At 5.07 ERA through 7.1 innings on the season.
